# MyDailyUpdater — LLM context file

Self-hosted daily research digest pipeline. Runs via GitHub Actions, outputs a
Jekyll static site (GitHub Pages) and optional Slack messages.

Source: https://github.com/YuyangXueEd/MyDailyUpdater
License: MIT

---

## Architecture overview

```
main.py  ──►  extensions/  ──►  publishers/  ──►  docs/
              (fetch → process     (JSON + Markdown
               → render → FeedSection)  templates)
                                              │
                                         sinks/  ──►  Slack
```

Entry point: `main.py`
- `--mode daily`   — full pipeline, commits output to docs/
- `--mode weekly`  — weekly rollup summary
- `--mode monthly` — monthly rollup summary
- `--dry-run`      — fetch only, zero LLM calls (for testing)

---

## Directory map

```
extensions/          Pluggable data source extensions (one package = one source)
  base.py            BaseExtension ABC + FeedSection dataclass
  __init__.py        REGISTRY list
  README.md          Full extension guide + PR checklist
  llms.txt           LLM context for extensions/ subtree
  arxiv/             arXiv papers: fetch → LLM score → summarise → enrich figures
    __init__.py      ArxivExtension class
    collector.py     fetch_papers(), enrich_papers_with_figures()
    scorer.py        score_papers() — LLM batch scoring
    summarizer.py    summarize_papers()
    README.md        Config options, output schema, test commands
    template.md.j2   Jinja2 section template
    head.html.j2     KaTeX CSS/JS injection for LaTeX rendering
    nav.md.j2        Per-category navigation links
  hacker_news/       HN stories above score threshold
    collector.py     fetch_stories()
  github_trending/   GitHub trending repos (no LLM scoring)
    collector.py     fetch_github_trending()
  weather/           Today's weather via Open-Meteo (no LLM)
    collector.py     fetch_today_weather()
  postdoc_jobs/      Research job listings (offline by default)
    collector.py     fetch_jobs()
  supervisor_updates/ Lab/advisor page change detection (offline by default)
  _template/         Starter template — copy this to build a new extension
    __init__.py      Fully documented extension skeleton
    collector.py     Fetch logic skeleton (separate from extension class)
    template.md.j2   Jinja2 section template skeleton
    README.md        6-section doc template + PR checklist

pipeline/            Shared utilities (not scorer/summariser — those live in extensions)
  config_loader.py   load_sources(), load_extension_config(name),
                     validate_sources(), validate_arxiv_config()
  aggregator.py      build_weekly_payload(), build_monthly_payload(),
                     load_daily_jsons(), compute_keyword_frequency()
  utils.py           lang_instruction(lang), parse_score(text),
                     call_llm_scoring(), call_llm_summarize()

publishers/
  data_publisher.py  build_daily_payload(), write_daily/weekly/monthly_json()
  pages_publisher.py render_daily/weekly/monthly_page() via Jinja2 templates

templates/           Top-level Jinja2 templates → Markdown pages
  daily.md.j2        Daily digest — iterates over all extension sections
  weekly.md.j2       Weekly rollup (hardcoded to top_papers, new_jobs — see debt)
  monthly.md.j2      Monthly overview (same)

sinks/               Delivery layer (all disabled by default)
  base.py            BaseSink ABC — enabled defaults False (opt-in)
  __init__.py        SINK_REGISTRY list
  README.md          Full sink guide
  slack/             SlackSink — Block Kit via Incoming Webhook
  _template/         Starter template for new sinks

config/
  sources.yaml       Enable/disable extensions, language, LLM models, sink config
  extensions/        Per-extension filter/keyword config
    arxiv.yaml       arXiv categories, must_include keywords, boost_keywords
    hacker_news.yaml HN score threshold and keyword filters
    weather.yaml     City and timezone overrides
    postdoc_jobs.yaml  Job search terms
    supervisor_updates.yaml  Pages to monitor

docs/                Generated site (served by GitHub Pages)
  daily/             Rendered Markdown pages
  data/daily/        Raw JSON payloads (YYYY-MM-DD.json)
  data/weekly/       Weekly JSON payloads
  data/monthly/      Monthly JSON payloads
  setup/             Static config wizard (index.html)

tests/               Pytest suite (60 tests, no live network or LLM calls)
```

---

## Key data schemas

### Daily payload (built by data_publisher.build_daily_payload)
```python
{
  "date": "2026-04-15",
  "generated_at": "2026-04-15T00:03:00+00:00",
  "papers": [...],           # list of paper dicts (see below)
  "hacker_news": [...],      # list of story dicts
  "github_trending": [...],  # list of repo dicts
  "weather": [...],          # list of weather item dicts
  "meta": {
    "papers_fetched": int,
    "papers_after_keyword_filter": int,
    "papers_after_llm_filter": int,
    "llm_model": str,
    "scoring_model": str,
    "summarization_model": str,
  }
}
```

### Paper dict (after full pipeline)
```python
{
  "id": "2604.12345",
  "title": str,
  "authors": list[str],
  "affiliations": list[str],
  "categories": list[str],          # e.g. ["cs.CV", "eess.IV"]
  "primary_category": str,
  "primary_category_anchor": str,   # URL-safe slug
  "url": str,                       # https://arxiv.org/abs/...
  "score": float,                   # LLM relevance score 0–10
  "abstract": str,                  # LLM-generated summary (in configured language)
  "keywords_matched": list[str],
  "figure_url": str | None,
  "figure_caption": str | None,
}
```

### HN story dict
```python
{
  "id": int, "title": str, "url": str,
  "score": int,           # HN points
  "comments_url": str,
  "summary": str,         # LLM one-liner
}
```

### GitHub repo dict
```python
{
  "full_name": str,        # "owner/repo"
  "url": str,
  "description": str,
  "language": str,
  "stars_today": int,
  "total_stars": int,
  "summary": str,          # LLM one-liner
}
```

---

## Extension contract

Every extension subclasses `BaseExtension` and implements:

```python
class MyExtension(BaseExtension):
    key   = "my_source"   # matches config/sources.yaml key
    title = "My Source"
    icon  = "🧩"          # shown in nav + section heading

    def fetch(self) -> list[dict]: ...          # no LLM; return raw items
    def process(self, items) -> list[dict]: ... # LLM ok; check dry_run
    def render(self, items) -> FeedSection: ... # no network
```

`self.config` keys injected by orchestrator:
- `enabled` — bool
- `language` — BCP-47 code, e.g. "en"
- `llm_scoring_model` — model name string
- `llm_summarization_model` — model name string
- `dry_run` — bool; when True skip all LLM calls

`self.llm` — OpenAI-compatible client (OpenRouter); use for chat completions.

Heavy fetch/parse logic goes in a separate `collector.py` inside the package so it
can be unit-tested without instantiating the extension class.

---

## Sink contract

```python
class MySink(BaseSink):
    key = "my_sink"   # matches sinks.my_sink in sources.yaml

    def deliver(self, payload: dict) -> None: ...
```

`enabled` defaults to `False` (opt-in). Credentials via `os.environ` only.
Sink exceptions are caught by orchestrator — they do not abort the pipeline.

---

## Config reference

### sources.yaml top-level keys
| Key | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `display_order` | list[str] | Order of sections in the rendered digest |
| `language` | str | BCP-47; LLM outputs in this language |
| `arxiv.enabled` | bool | |
| `arxiv.max_papers_per_run` | int | Papers fetched before keyword filter |
| `hacker_news.enabled` | bool | |
| `github_trending.enabled` | bool | |
| `github_trending.max_repos` | int | |
| `weather.enabled` | bool | |
| `weather.city` | str | City name for geocoding |
| `weather.timezone` | str | IANA timezone or "auto" |
| `llm.scoring_model` | str | OpenRouter model ID |
| `llm.summarization_model` | str | OpenRouter model ID |
| `llm.base_url` | str | Default: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 |
| `sinks.slack.enabled` | bool | Requires SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL env var |

### config/extensions/arxiv.yaml keys
| Key | Notes |
|---|---|
| `categories` | arXiv category codes, e.g. cs.CV |
| `must_include` | At least one keyword must match title/abstract |
| `boost_keywords` | Raise LLM score if matched |
| `llm_score_threshold` | Papers below this score (0–10) are dropped |

---

## LLM usage patterns

All LLM calls use `self.llm.chat.completions.create(model=..., messages=[...])`.
Language is injected via `lang_instruction(lang)` from `pipeline/utils.py`:
- Returns `"in English"` for `lang="en"`
- Returns `"in Chinese (Simplified) (non-English)"` for `lang="zh"`, etc.

Scoring uses batch prompts (multiple papers per request) for efficiency.
Summarisation is per-item, done in each extension's `summarizer.py`.

---

## Known tech debt

- `templates/weekly.md.j2` and `templates/monthly.md.j2` hardcode `top_papers`,
  `new_jobs`, `trending_keywords`. They are not extension-aware — adding a new
  extension requires manual template edits for weekly/monthly views.

---

## Testing

```bash
PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/ -q          # 60 tests, ~3s, no network/LLM
python main.py --dry-run               # live fetch, zero LLM cost
```

Tests cover parsing/filtering logic with inline fixture data. No live API calls.
See `tests/conftest.py` for shared fixtures.
